title: Nifedipine: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:
edicine 
Nifedipine is also used sometimes to treat preterm labor and Raynaud's syndrome. Talk to your doctor about the possible risks of using this medication for your condition.
This medication is sometimes prescribed for other uses; ask your doctor or pharmacist for more information.
What special precautions should I follow? 
Before taking nifedipine, tell your doctor and pharmacist if you are allergic to nifedipine, any other medications, or any of the ingredients in nifedipine. Ask your pharmacist for a list of the ingredients. some medications should not be taken with nifedipine. Other medications may cause dosing changes or extra monitoring when taken with nifedipine. Make sure you have discussed any medications you are currently taking or plan to take before starting nifedipine with your doctor and pharmacist. Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking nifedipine, please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist.. the following nonprescription or herbal products may interact with nifedipine: St. John's wort; cimetidine (Tagamet). Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking nifedipine. Do not start any of these medications while taking nifedipine without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you have or have ever had a narrowing or blockage of your digestiv
